notes: Confirmed: SKGLView.Dispose() is declared as 'public void Dispose()' (non-virtual) in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, preventing subclasses from overriding disposal behavior. The same pattern exists in SKCanvasView. Tested on versions 2.88.9 and 3.119.2 — both exhibit the same gap. Main branch source also shows no change. The standard .NET Dispose(bool) pattern (CA1063) would resolve this.

reproductionSteps.description: Create class library project and add SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.9
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new classlib -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 2.88.9
reproductionSteps.output:
Restore succeeded.
PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '2.88.9' added.

filesCreated.description: Subclass of SKGLView attempting to override Dispose to demonstrate the non-virtual gap

reproductionSteps.description: Build project with SKGLView subclass attempting to override Dispose (v2.88.9)
reproductionSteps.output:
error CS0506: 'MySKGLView.Dispose()': cannot override inherited member 'SKGLView.Dispose()' because it is not marked virtual, abstract, or override

Build failed with 1 error(s) in 1.0s

reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new classlib -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.2 && dotnet build
reproductionSteps.output:
error CS0506: 'MySKGLView.Dispose()': cannot override inherited member 'SKGLView.Dispose()' because it is not marked virtual, abstract, or override

Build failed with 1 error(s) in 1.3s

reproductionSteps.description: Investigate main branch source for SKGLView.Dispose() and SKCanvasView.Dispose()
reproductionSteps.output:
SKGLView.razor.cs:190:		public void Dispose()
SKCanvasView.razor.cs:171:		public void Dispose()

versionResults.notes: CS0506: Dispose is non-virtual, cannot override in subclass
CS0506: Same non-virtual Dispose, gap persists in latest stable
Source grep confirms Dispose() is still non-virtual on main branch; same pattern in SKCanvasView

actionability.reason: Enhancement confirmed across all versions including main. The fix is straightforward (implement Dispose(bool) pattern per CA1063) but needs implementation and testing.

workarounds: Shadow the Dispose method using 'new' keyword: public new void Dispose() { /* custom cleanup */ base.Dispose(); } — note this breaks polymorphic disposal through IDisposable references
